survey: US Mineral Survey No. 52 (Garfield lode), T.29N R.100W 6th P.M., Wyoming, 1892
note: License-free correctness key derived from the claim's OWN geometry: a lode claim is a surveyed rectangle, so its boundary courses must satisfy exact structural constraints. A blind read of the plat is validated by checking these invariants — no external answer key needed, and contamination-proof. Strongest for high-entropy (non-cardinal) bearings, where coincidental agreement is astronomically unlikely.

invariants.name: opposite_ends_equal
invariants.check: both end lines read 600 ft, anti-parallel
